title: Verilator not truncating c++ variable when passing to verilog module

features.name: narrow input assignment from large C++ value
features.description: Code should include a narrow Verilog module input (5 bits) assigned from a C++ value that exceeds the input width (0x30), where the higher bits should be truncated

features.name: concatenation extending bit width before shift
features.description: Code should include a concatenation that extends a value's bit width by appending bits, creating a wider wire (33 bits from 32 bits plus 1 bit)

features.name: shift on extended wire with narrow shift amount
features.description: Code should include a left shift operation on an extended-width wire (33 bits) using a narrow shift amount input (5 bits), with result sliced to original width
features.snippet:
wire [32:0] extend = { val, 1'b0 };
assign out = {extend << sham}[32:1];
